title: xtra crispy egyptian wonton skin gyoza
description: savory, snappy, energizing egyptian dish featuring wonton skin, robata-grill, lacto-vegetarian-friendly
instructions: wrap each wonton skin around a small amount of the chopped onion and garlic mixture, sealing the edges with a small amount of water.
brush the gyoza with olive oil and sprinkle with egyptian spice mix, salt, and black pepper.
place the gyoza on the preheated robata-grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side or until crispy and golden brown.
repeat with remaining gyoza, adjusting cooking time as necessary.
serve the xtra crispy egyptian wonton skin gyoza hot with a side of chopped cilantro and fava beans.
store any leftover gyoza in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
reheat leftover gyoza in a pan with a small amount of oil over medium heat for 2-3 minutes or until crispy and heated through.
for an added crunch, sprinkle the gyoza with cornstarch and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes on each side before serving.
to make the chopped onion and garlic mixture, finely chop 1/2 cup of onions and 2 cloves of garlic and mix with 1/4 cup of chopped cilantro and 1/2 cup of crushed fava beans.
to make the egyptian spice mix, combine 1 teaspoon of ground cumin, 1/2 teaspoon of ground coriander, and 1/4 teaspoon of cayenne pepper in a small bowl.
to store the wonton skins, place them in an airtight container and keep in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or freeze for up to 3 months.
